# Palimpsesto

> Project memory for coding agents that never erases the *why*. Durable Markdown
> holds the truth; a local semantic engine (ChromaDB + a SQLite synaptic graph)
> recalls it. If you are an AI agent working in a project that uses Palimpsesto,
> this file teaches you everything needed to set it up and operate it.

Palimpsesto is two layers, and **both are the tool**:

1. **Durable files** — curated Markdown under a `memory/` directory, indexed by
   `MEMORY.md`. Each memory that evolves carries `compiled_truth` (rewritable)
   and `timeline` (append-only). This is the source of truth.
2. **Semantic engine** (`semantic/`) — a local ChromaDB vector store + a SQLite
   Hebbian synaptic graph. It runs on-device (zero API cost, no vendor) and is
   what turns a folder of notes into *recall*. It is not optional.

Golden rule: **never overwrite a conclusion silently.** When understanding
changes, rewrite `compiled_truth` AND append a `reversal` line to `timeline`,
together — so the reasoning behind a change of mind stays on the record.

## Setup (do this once, in order)

1. Install the skill + engine:
   ```sh
   ./setup.sh                 # links the skill into ~/.claude, ~/.codex; builds semantic/.venv with deps
   ./setup.sh ~/proj/memory   # ...and scaffolds a memory dir (PALIMPSESTO.md, _TEMPLATE.md, MEMORY.md)
   ```
   Requires Python 3 for the engine. Deps: `chromadb`, `mcp`, `pyyaml`.

2. Register the MCP server so `save_context` / `search_context` / `strengthen_synapses`
   are callable. Add to `~/.claude.json` (or a project `.mcp.json`):
   ```json
   { "mcpServers": { "memory": {
       "command": "/ABS/PATH/semantic/.venv/bin/python",
       "args": ["/ABS/PATH/semantic/server.py"] } } }
   ```

3. Wire the hooks so recall + capture happen automatically (`~/.claude/settings.json`):
   ```json
   { "hooks": {
       "UserPromptSubmit": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command",
         "command": "/ABS/PATH/semantic/.venv/bin/python /ABS/PATH/semantic/hook_retrieve.py" } ] } ],
       "Stop": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command",
         "command": "/ABS/PATH/semantic/.venv/bin/python /ABS/PATH/semantic/hook_autosave.py" } ] } ] } }
   ```
   - `hook_retrieve.py` (UserPromptSubmit): vector search + synaptic neighbors, injected into your context before you answer.
   - `hook_autosave.py` (Stop): distills the last turns into a compact memory and saves it; also runs light maintenance ~1×/day.

4. Seed the engine from the durable files so curated memory is searchable:
   ```sh
   semantic/.venv/bin/python semantic/seed_from_memory.py /ABS/PATH/memory --dry   # preview
   semantic/.venv/bin/python semantic/seed_from_memory.py /ABS/PATH/memory         # write
   ```

## Writing a memory (the durable layer)

Create one file per durable unit of knowledge under `memory/`. Frontmatter:

```
---
name: <kebab-case-slug>
description: <one-line summary used for recall>
metadata:
  type: user | feedback | project | reference
---
```

- `user` / `reference` are stable — a plain body is enough.
- `project` / `feedback` evolve — use the two-layer body:
  ```
  # <title>
  ## compiled_truth
  <current best understanding — rewrite this whole section as it improves>
  ## timeline
  - <YYYY-MM-DD> · <kind> · <one line> [· source: <origin>]
  **Why:** <reasoning>
  **How to apply:** <what to do with this>
  ```
- `kind` ∈ `decision | reversal | hypothesis | finding | milestone | correction`.
- Add ONE pointer line to `MEMORY.md`: `- [Title](file.md) — one-line hook`.
  `MEMORY.md` holds pointers only, never content; it is read every session.
- Link related memories with `[[name]]` (another file's `name:` slug).
- The test for what belongs: *"Will this still matter in months, and is it hard
  to reconstruct from the code?"* Yes → write it. No → leave it in code.

## Recall (the semantic engine)

- **Automatic:** with the hooks wired, relevant memories are injected at the
  start of each turn under a `[semantic-memory]` block; substantial turns are
  auto-saved on Stop. You do not call anything by hand for this.
- **Manual (MCP tools):** available via the `memory` server —
  - `save_context(content, tags?, source?, session_id?, role?)` — store a distilled insight (prefer summaries over raw text).
  - `search_context(query, n_results?, min_score?, source?, tags?)` — semantic search.
  - `strengthen_synapses(memory_ids, delta?)` — after using 2+ memories together, wire them so spreading activation surfaces them together next time.
  - `list_sources()` / `synapse_stats()` — introspection. `delete_context(doc_id)` — remove one.
- **Spreading activation:** a strong vector hit pulls in its synaptic neighbors,
  so relationally-relevant context surfaces even when it isn't textually similar.
  Co-retrieved memories auto-strengthen; unused links decay and get pruned daily.

## Operating rules (do / don't)

- DO capture decisions and corrections the moment they surface, not at the end.
- DO append a `reversal`/`correction` to `timeline` when a past call is overturned; never delete the old reasoning.
- DO retrofit the `compiled_truth` + `timeline` shape into a file the next time you touch it — never rewrite history in bulk.
- DO treat a memory as point-in-time: if it cites a file/line/value, verify against current code before asserting it as fact.
- DON'T commit real memory to the Palimpsesto repo — `memory/`, `semantic/data/`, `semantic/.venv/` are git-ignored.
- DON'T store secrets or credentials in memory files or the semantic store.
- DON'T put memory content in `MEMORY.md` — pointers only.

## File map

- `PALIMPSESTO.md` — the durable-layer contract (read this to learn the memory format).
- `templates/` — `MEMORY.md` (index seed), `page.md` (blank), `page-example.md` (worked reversal).
- `skills/palimpsesto/SKILL.md` — the install-once skill teaching the convention.
- `semantic/` — the engine: `server.py` (MCP), `synapses.py` (SQLite graph), `hook_retrieve.py`, `hook_autosave.py`, `maintenance.py`, `sleep_cycle.py`, `seed_from_memory.py`.
- `setup.sh` — installer; `--no-semantic` (files only), `--uninstall` (`--purge` also deletes data).

## Reference

- [PALIMPSESTO.md](https://github.com/jeferson-guedes/Palimpsesto/blob/main/PALIMPSESTO.md): the durable-layer contract in full.
- [semantic/README.md](https://github.com/jeferson-guedes/Palimpsesto/blob/main/semantic/README.md): the engine, install, and wiring in detail.
- [README.md](https://github.com/jeferson-guedes/Palimpsesto/blob/main/README.md): overview and quickstart.
- Credit: adapts `compiled_truth` + append-only `timeline` from [brain.md](https://github.com/mindmuxai/brain.md) (MindMux), kept as a convention.
